title: Name/secure domain-expert + editorial reviewers (blocking gate roles)
context: food-security-briefs requires three independent, non-skippable review gates for every brief: a domain/statistical expert, an editorial (non-alarmist) reviewer, and a licence/provenance reviewer. Until at least one named, qualified reviewer is secured for each gate, no brief can proceed to review or publication. These roles are explicitly blocking — the M0 pilot cannot ship without them.
objective: Identify and confirm named individuals willing to serve as domain-expert reviewer, editorial reviewer, and licence/provenance reviewer, and document their credentials and contact channels.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one named domain/statistical expert (food security analyst, agricultural economist, or public-health nutrition specialist with relevant credentials) agrees to review briefs and is documented with contact channel.
At least one named editorial reviewer agrees to apply the non-alarmist style guide checklist and is documented.
At least one named licence/provenance reviewer agrees to verify CC-BY-IGO attribution and non-endorsement compliance and is documented.
Reviewer commitments recorded in a committed document (e.g. docs/reviewers.md or equivalent) with name, role, credentials summary, and contact channel.
All roles filled before the M0 pilot (pilot-008) enters review; until then, all brief tasks remain verifiedNeed: false and no brief ships.
resources: PLAN.md — Quality, review & risk gates section
output: A committed document recording the names, roles, credentials, and contact channels of the secured domain-expert, editorial, and licence/provenance reviewers.
